sources.title: Ten Gentlemen from West Point 1942 colorized (George Montgomery)
sources.description: The West Point military academy, which trained officers for the American Revolutionary War, is re-established in the early 19th century on a trial basis, prompted by the efforts of a young Washington socialite (Maureen O'Hara). The new commandant (Laird Cregar), who doesn't believe a college can produce real fighting men, is determined to enforce strict discipline on the small band of cadets, which include the socialite's fiancée (John Sutton) and a Kentucky frontiersman (George Montgomery), who compete for her affections. Many of the cadets drop out under the discouraging conditions, until only ten are left when they have to fight an Indian uprising. George Montgomery as Joe Dawson Maureen O'Hara as Carolyn Bainbridge John Sutton as Howard Shelton Laird Cregar as Maj. Sam Carter Shepperd Strudwick as Henry Clay Victor Francen as Florimond Massey Harry Davenport as Bane Ward Bond as Sgt. Scully Douglass Dumbrille as Gen. William Henry Harrison Ralph Byrd as Maloney If you have a request or a comment for me, please go to the Colorized Movies Forum at https://archive.org/details/colorized-movies?tab=forum Icolorized this to the best of my ability using the best quality black & white copy I could find, using AI software that doesn't always get the colors right, but I think it looks fairly natural. Hopefully. someday it will be remastered and a more professional colorizing job will be done.

metadata.Plot: Among those who are fighting to have Congress re-establish the military academy at West Point in the beginning of the nineteenth century is a young Washington socialite, Carolyn Bainbridge. Congress resolves to revive the Academy on a one-year trial basis. Major Sam Carter, a martinet who doesn't believe a college can produce real fighting men, is made the Commandant, and determines to make soldiers - or failures - out of the small band of cadets, by enforcing stringent disciplinary action. Among the cadets are Howard Shelton, Carolyn's fiancée, and Dawson, a Kentucky frontiersman. There is bad blood between the two from the start, and matters are worsened when Dawson falls in love with Carolyn. Many of the cadets resign, under the discouraging conditions and grueling punishment that is part of Carter's plan to make the school hard and the exercises difficult, and the number of cadets left is down to ten. Word arrives that the Indian chief Tecumseh has gone on the warpath, and Carter is ordered to take part in quelling the rebellion, and to include the cadets along with the regular Bombadiers. Shelton, on patrol, is shot at by an Indian, and hot-headedly leaves his post in pursuit. Dawson leaves to bring him back followed by Major Carter who is captured by the Indians. Using tactics learned at West Point, the small band of cadets attack and demoralize the much larger Indian force, and succeed in rescuing Carter, and Dawson is wounded. Carter is grateful for the actions taken by the band, but says that Shelton and Dawson cannot remain as cadets since they had violated the rules by leaving their posts, and would be a bad precedent for future cadets. Carolyn has come to the Point to marry the unsuspecting Dawson, who thought she loved Shelton.
metadata.Writer: Ben Hecht, Talbot Jennings, Richard Maibaum
metadata.Actors: George Montgomery, Maureen O'Hara, John Sutton
